reasoning: Gallup is the official resolution source. The most recently available Gallup measurement (from May 1-18, 2025) placed Trump’s approval at 43%. In March 2025, his Gallup approval was 42%, and apart from a brief spike at his inauguration, his numbers have remained rigidly sub-45% in his second term due to hyper-polarization (he retains ~90% Republican but under 5% Democrat approval). Mid-June polls from other outlets (like Quinnipiac at 38% and Reuters/Ipsos at 42%) indicate his approval has either remained stagnant or dipped recently amidst nationwide protests and polarizing policy moves. For him to score strictly >45% (so 46% or higher) on August 1st would require an unexpected rally event to swing Independents significantly within just 5.5 weeks. Given this historically rare volatility and lack of upward momentum, the chances are extremely low.
